creator: kreinholz@gmail.com
text:
It's not so much a bug with either abseil or protobuf as it is an ABI incompatibility across different versions of llvm. (That seems to hit a small number of packages).

For synth (and poudriere, it would seem) users, disabling pre-fetching and ccache, then rebuilding abseil (`synth force devel/abseil`) before attempting to build protobuf results in a successful build. Once built one time with updated system llvm, you can re-enable ccache and it will continue to build future protobuf updates fine. (This is my current setup, and I had no issues after the most recent version bump to protobuf since my locally built abseil was ABI compatible).

The problem with pre-fetching as I understand it is that the official package build servers build on the lowest possible freebsd-14: 14.0-RELEASE. So if pre-fetching is enabled, it pulls in abseil built with llvm lower than version 18, where the ABI breakage occurs.

This doesn't just affect abseil and protobuf. I've experienced similar breakages with other packages. It's uncommon, but it's by no means limited to just these.

Best practice (or at least, what I do now) is after updating my freebsd-stable version to a commit with a new major version of llvm (such as 17 -> 18, or 18 -> 19), disable ccache and rebuild packages. Then re-enable ccache to speed up future builds. At no time do I enable pre-fetching; as tempting as it is to speed up builds, I no longer trust it to do the right thing. Sometimes it pulls in ABI incompatible versions of dependencies like abseil, causing surprise build breakages like this one.

This is basically another way of repeating the often heard FreeBSD mantra of "never mix ports and packages." Pre-fetching works fine until it doesn't.
